analysis.problem: Lack of regulatory framework for space mining and resource utilization
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient international cooperation and agreement on space resource utilization policies
leverage_points: International Space Agency cooperation and policy development
Private sector investment in space resource utilization technologies

proposed_actions.action: Establish an international framework for space resource utilization
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Develop and ratify an international treaty on space resource utilization within 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Invest in research and development of sustainable space resource utilization technologies
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase private sector investment in space resource utilization by 20% annually for the next 3 years

evidence: The Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015 was introduced but never became law
The European Space Resources Innovation Centre was established to promote space resource utilization
Private companies such as Planetary Resources are investing in asteroid mining technologies
analysis.generalization: The lack of a regulatory framework for space mining and resource utilization connects to other domains, such as the development of sustainable technologies and the need for international cooperation on environmental issues
analysis.summary: The lack of a regulatory framework for space mining and resource utilization poses a medium-level problem, which can be addressed through international cooperation and private sector investment. Establishing an international framework and investing in sustainable technologies can help mitigate this issue.

raw_items.title: Asteroid mining
raw_items.summary: Asteroid mining is the hypothetical and technically possible extraction of materials from asteroids and other minor planets, including near-Earth objects.Research missions focused on asteroid sample return, including Hayabusa, Hayabusa2, OSIRIS-REx, and Tianwen-2, illustrate the challenges of collecting ore from space using current technology. raw_items.title: Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015
raw_items.summary: The Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015 (H.R. 1508) was a bill (which never became law) introduced in the 114th Congress of the U.S. on March 19, 2015, by Rep. Bill Posey, Republican of Florida. It is identical to the Senate bill by the same name (S. 976).
